summary: Rule chunk from Paranoia for Players page 28, chunk 5.

mechanics.raw_text:
‘Another clever ploy is providing narration for the record tapes by speaking loudly as the events are recorded, 
“Note the diligent labor of citizen Burnm- R as he searches for the source of the jackobot's malfunction.” (Bumm-R is in fact, reprogramming the bot to respond to a remote control self-destruct device that will obviate the need for an extensive debriefing by ‘obliterating the other party members and the briefing personnel.) : 
‘When Disaster Looms, Keep Stalling 
— Only Heroes Are Too Stupid to Wait Patiently for Miracles When called upon to perform the impossi- ble in the face of certain death, we always politely ask The Computer if we can goto the bathroom first — and if we can have a glass cof water — and if we can check just one more reference by data search — and if we can field- strip the electron howitzer to find that buzz- ing nose it makes when you st the fuse timer 1d so on — and so forth. This tactic is most effective if the requests can be made privately and without alerting the ret of the mission fr0up to the fact that you will be tarrying behind just a litle bit. Once they catch on, ‘everyone gets into the ac, and The Computer's temper simulation software gets short Stalling is also the ideal tactic for resisting the peremptory orders of superiors, "Of course, si right away, I'm right on top of it, just have to get my tools ready, yess, et, could you stand back abit so I can spread these out 6n the floor and make sure all of ther are neatly sorted by size, shape, color, and serial number ‘Of course, stalling doesn't make the loom ing disaster go away.
